title: Callista rotundata
taxonomy: Callista rotundata belongs to the family Cardiidae, which is a group of marine bivalve mollusks commonly known as cockles. This species is a type of saltwater clam found in tropical and subtropical regions. More specifically, it is classified within the genus Callista, which contains various species of clams. The classification of Callista rotundata is further divided into the kingdom Animalia, phylum Mollusca, and class Bivalvia. This classification system helps to organize and understand the complex relationships within the natural world.
uses: Callista rotundata is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Africa, where it is valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is said to have various medicinal uses, including treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its roots and leaves are used in the treatment of different ailments. The plant is also used to treat wounds and infections. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of Callista rotundata.

side_effects: Callista rotundata has been reported to have potential side effects due to its toxicity. The consumption of this plant can cause gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea and vomiting, as well as kidney damage in severe cases. Ingestion may also lead to symptoms of neurological impairment.
